title: Gliricidia brenningii
intro: Gliricidia brenningii is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Gliricidia brenningii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Gliricidia brenningii is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Gliricidia. The genus Gliricidia is a part of the tribe Ingeae, which is a group of legumes native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is also known as the 'Mexican tree' due to its geographical distribution. The classification of Gliricidia brenningii reflects its close evolutionary relationship with other legume species.
